safe_public_summary: Visiting clinical electives at UPMC are governed by the University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine (UPSOM) — a system-level program. Site-specific availability at Western Psychiatric Hospital is not separately enumerated by the source.
required_caveats: System-level UPSOM/UPMC source — Western Psychiatric site placement not separately enumerated;Domestic visiting program: LCME / AOA accredited home institutions in North America only;International visiting program: enrolled international students in their final year of medical education only — graduates not eligible;Application: VSLO or paper via MSRIS@medschool.pitt.edu;USD 4500 per clinical elective (international program);USMLE Step 2 required to apply for a Psychiatric clinical elective;B-1/B-2 visa documentation supported on acceptance — J-1 / H-1B sponsorship not stated on source
audience_allowed: US MD students at LCME-accredited home institutions (domestic);US DO students at AOA-accredited home institutions (domestic);International medical students enrolled in final year of medical education (international program)
audience_excluded: Caribbean medical students for the domestic program (not LCME/AOA NA accredited);Medical graduates seeking observerships or externships (international program excludes graduates);Students not in their final year of medical education (international program)
visa_wording: B-1/B-2 visa documentation supported on acceptance — J-1 / H-1B sponsorship not stated
application_wording: VSLO or paper via MSRIS@medschool.pitt.edu — paper preferred
site_specificity_wording: System-level UPSOM/UPMC source — Western Psychiatric site placement not separately enumerated
banned_claims: guaranteed Western Psychiatric rotation;hospital-approved USCEHub listing;apply through USCEHub;IMG-friendly pathway;Caribbean accessible (domestic program);J-1 sponsorship;H-1B sponsorship;observership for graduates;general broad national audience
notes: Display bucket on rendered card should mirror CC Hillcrest framing. The 'open to international students per source' label applies only with the international-program-final-year-only carveout.

safe_public_summary: Lincoln is one site within NYC Health + Hospitals; visiting medical-student access flows through the system-level MOSAIC Visiting Scholars Program. A specialty-specific Emergency Medicine MS3/4 rotation page also exists at the Lincoln Emergency Medicine residency site.
required_caveats: System-level NYC H+H MOSAIC source — Lincoln site placement not separately guaranteed;Eligibility: U.S. LCME-accredited allopathic medical students or AOA-accredited osteopathic medical students only;Caribbean / IMG students explicitly excluded by accreditation language;Application by PDF email to MOSAIC@nychhc.org;$2,000 stipend per four-week rotation + $2,000 housing stipend for participants outside the NYC metro area;Lincoln Emergency Medicine site-specific page covers Emergency Medicine MS3/4 rotation (4 weeks); audience accreditation, fees, and visa policy silent on this page — do not broaden
audience_allowed: US MD students at LCME-accredited home institutions;US DO students at AOA-accredited home institutions;URM students encouraged to apply (per source)
audience_excluded: Caribbean medical students (not LCME/AOA);International medical graduates (not LCME/AOA);Medical graduates seeking observerships
visa_wording: N/A — US-only audience; visa policy not relevant per source
application_wording: PDF email to MOSAIC@nychhc.org with CV/transcripts/personal statement/letter of recommendation
cost_wording: $2,000 stipend per rotation + $2,000 housing stipend for non-NYC metro applicants
site_specificity_wording: System-level NYC H+H MOSAIC source — Lincoln site placement not separately guaranteed; Lincoln EM secondary source covers EM specialty only
banned_claims: open to IMGs;open to Caribbean students;guaranteed Lincoln placement (system-level program);all specialties (EM source is specialty-restricted);visa sponsorship;apply through USCEHub;hospital-approved USCEHub listing;USCEHub affiliation
notes: Display bucket on rendered card should be 'US MD/DO per source' — NOT 'open to international students per source'. Lincoln EM secondary source is supplementary; primary source for audience/eligibility is MOSAIC.
